text: {"matches":[{"type":"narrower","fromIndex":10,"toIndex":1,"rationale":"Claim 10 gives a precise cholesterol amount (186 mg) which is a specific instance of the general statement that eggs are high in cholesterol."},{"type":"narrower","fromIndex":11,"toIndex":9,"rationale":"Claim 11 provides a conditional recommendation (one egg per day if other cholesterol is low), which is a specific case within the broader guideline to keep total dietary cholesterol under 300 mg per day."},{"type":"compatible-different-scope","fromIndex":5,"toIndex":6,"rationale":"Claim 5 refers to healthy individuals, while claim 6 concerns people with diabetes; the two statements can both be true for their respective populations."},{"type":"contradicts","fromIndex":6,"toIndex":7,"rationale":"Claim 6 says seven eggs per week increase heart disease risk in diabetics, whereas claim 7 reports research finding no such connection, directly opposing each other."},{"type":"compatible-different-scope","fromIndex":3,"toIndex":4,"rationale":"Claim 3 attributes heart‑disease risk mainly to foods eaten with eggs, while claim 4 attributes it mainly to cooking methods; both can be true because they address different contributing factors."}]}
